fields.description:
## Context

The reconciler coordinates concurrent passes across clones (local + the GitHub-Actions
reconcile bridge) with a **pass-lock** and a **phase-gate**. Today both are **working-tree
files committed to the shared `tickets` orphan branch**:
`.reconciler-pass-lock` and `.reconciler-phase-gate` (`_advisory_lock.py:69-70`), written by
`acquire_pass_lock` (`_advisory_lock.py:243`) via `_write_file_to_tickets_branch`, deleted by
`release_pass_lock` (`_advisory_lock.py:302`), and orchestrated in the reconciler entrypoint
(`rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:373-447`). The lock body is just `pass_id\ntimestamp_ns`.

Two problems with a tree-committed lock:

1. **It rides the union-merge / `merge=ours` band-aid.** `.reconciler-*` is matched by the
   `merge=ours` driver in the tickets-branch `.gitattributes` (installed by
   `_commands/init.py:55-56`). Two clones that touch the lock on diverged histories reconverge
   by silently keeping one side — the lock is coordination state living in a store whose merge
   policy is "drop a write."
2. **No self-healing.** There is no lease/heartbeat/steal. A pass that **crashes while holding
   the lock** leaves a stale file that only clears by ownership-checked delete on the next clean
   run. The current mitigation is the `b859-8fa1` **retry band-aid** — an outer backoff+jitter
   retry loop (`_advisory_lock.py:73-89, 263-291`) that papers over write contention rather than
   resolving stale holders.

**Recent ground truth that motivates doing this now.** `feat/reconcile-continuous-loop` is
**merged to main** (flag-gated behind the `RECONCILE_CONTINUOUS` GitHub Variable, default off).
Its commit explicitly relies on "concurrency + the reconciler pass-lock" to prevent overlapping
passes — i.e. it is the contention amplifier this workstream hardens against. As the reconciler
moves toward a continuous cadence, a stale/crashed-holder lock and merge-policy-dropped lock
writes become live operational risks, not theoretical ones.

**This epic is the WS-2 (coordination) half of the former "conflict-free reconciler state"
epic.** WS-1 (event-sourcing the binding *data*) has been **dropped**: the closed epic
*Level-triggered bridge convergence* (`3006-e198-13db-4e1f`, ADRs 0026-0029 + the `8de5`
classifier/canary) already made binding drift **detected and self-healing** (`classify()` +
`bridge_fsck` audit + the `reconcile-bridge-canary` CI alarm), and `bindings.json` is a largely
**derived cache** the next pass rebuilds — so the "silent binding data-loss" premise no longer
justifies a high-risk rewrite of the just-stabilized identity store. This epic is deliberately
scoped to the **lock/gate coordination state**, which is genuinely non-reconstructible and
untouched by `3006`.

**Framed honestly as an EFFICIENCY lock, not a correctness lock.** Reconcile passes are
idempotent; the lock exists to avoid wasted overlapping work, not to protect correctness. That
is why moving it to a self-healing, occasionally-stealable ref is safe: a rare double-acquire
degrades to two idempotent passes (which the `3006` `classify()` matrix + acting-fraction breaker
already tolerate), never to corruption.

## Design (WS-2 — self-healing ref-lock)

- Move the pass-lock and phase-gate OFF the working tree into a **bare git ref**:
  `refs/reconciler/lock` (and gate), ref -> blob. Not in the tickets tree, so never union-merged
  and never subject to `merge=ours`. Authoritative ref lives on `origin` (the `sync.remote`).
- **Create-only CAS acquire** and **CAS release** over the ref, reusing the existing ref-CAS
  primitive already in `_advisory_lock.py:92-111` (the `git update-ref refs/... <new> <old>`
  compare-and-swap used for advancing the tickets branch) rather than inventing a new one. Over
  the wire use explicit `--force-with-lease=<ref>:<old>` — never the bare lease form.
- **Lock blob** carries: holder id + a **relative** lease duration (clock-skew-proof, the
  DynamoDB-lock-client pattern) + an HLC heartbeat + a monotonic fence counter (diagnostic).
- **Self-heal:** the holder renews the lease by heartbeat at ~lease/3; a contender may
  **CAS-break only an expired lock**, and the CAS guarantees exactly one breaker wins a
  double-break race.
- Once the ref-lock lands, **remove the `b859` retry band-aid** and **remove `.reconciler-*`
  from the `merge=ours` `.gitattributes`**; assert no `.reconciler-*` file is ever committed to
  the tickets branch again.

Note: `.ticket-write.lock` (`_store/lock.py:36`, a local fcntl+mkdir lock for single-host ticket
writes acquired by `inbound_translate.py:210`) is a **separate** mechanism — NOT cross-clone pass
coordination — and is explicitly out of scope.

## Success Criteria

- The reconciler coordinates concurrent passes with zero coordination state in the tickets
  working tree, and a crashed pass-holder never wedges the reconciler beyond one lease interval.
- The `merge=ours` policy no longer covers any live coordination state.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pass-lock and phase-gate live in `refs/reconciler/*` (bare ref -> blob, not in the working
      tree, not union-merged); acquire is create-only CAS, release is CAS
      (`_engine/rebar_reconciler/` — new ref-lock module + `_advisory_lock.py`,
      `rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`).
- [ ] Lock blob carries holder + relative lease duration + HLC heartbeat + monotonic fence
      counter; the holder renews at ~lease/3.
- [ ] Stale-lock self-heal: a contender CAS-breaks only an expired lease; a double-break race
      resolves to exactly one winner (regression test).
- [ ] `.reconciler-*` removed from the `merge=ours` `.gitattributes` (`_commands/init.py:55-56`)
      and its test (`tests/unit/store/test_gitattributes_merge.py`); no `.reconciler-*` file is
      committed to the tickets branch (regression test).
- [ ] The `b859-8fa1` lock-retry band-aid (`_advisory_lock.py:73-89, 263-291`) is removed once
      the self-healing lock supersedes it.
- [ ] Reconcile-pass idempotency preserved/asserted (the invariant that makes an efficiency lock
      safe); reuse the existing ref-CAS primitive rather than adding a parallel one.
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green.
fields.title: Self-healing reconciler pass-lock: move lock/gate to refs/reconciler/* (retire merge=ours + retry band-aid)
